title: Getting started with roxyassert
text: roxyassert reads structured type annotations roxygen2 documentation , document() time, generates per-function argument return assertion helpers. generated checks calls assert package, function’s documented contract runtime validation come one source. See README full annotation grammar; vignette short tour.

code: #' Submit an order. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) normalised `BASE/QUOTE` pair. #' @param quantity (scalar<numeric>) order size. #' @param price_limit (scalar<numeric>?) limit price; `NULL` for market orders. #' @return (data.table) the accepted order: #' - **order_id** (character): exchange order id. #' - **quantity** (numeric): accepted size. #' - **datetime** (POSIXct): acceptance time. #' @export submit_order <- function(symbol, quantity, price_limit = NULL) {   assert_args_submit_order(symbol, quantity, price_limit)   # generated   result <- ...   return(assert_return_submit_order(result))                # generated }

what: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Write contract — roxygen2 docs — generate runtime checks. roxyassert reads structured type annotations roxygen2 documentation , document() time, generates per-function assertion helpers validate function’s arguments return value. generated checks plain calls assert package, written committed source file can read debug. point: already describe parameter’s type return shape @param / @return docs. Today also hand-write separate validation function repeats — two drift. roxyassert makes documentation single source truth: docs render humans generate assertions, can never disagree.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: roxyassert roxygen2 roclet: runs documentation step (one writes NAMESPACE man/*.Rd), never R CMD build/check. parses tag text — never executes code — writes one file commit alongside rest generated artifacts. Layering: assert dependency roxyassert stays lightweight; roxyassert depends assert + roxygen2.
code: R/*.R  (your code + typed roxygen tags)      |      |   devtools::document()   <- roxyassert runs here, as a roclet      v   R/contracts-generated.R       <- generated assert_args_* / assert_return_* helpers      |      |   you call them inside your functions, and commit the file      v   runtime validation via the `assert` package

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Register roclet package’s DESCRIPTION: ’s entire integration. Now devtools::document() runs roxyassert alongside built-roclets (re)writes R/contracts-generated.R.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: document(), R/contracts-generated.R contains (illustrative):
code: #' Submit an order. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) normalised `BASE/QUOTE` pair. #' @param side (enum<character, ORDER_SIDE>) order side. #' @param quantity (scalar<numeric>) order size in the base asset. #' @param price_limit (scalar<numeric>?) limit price; `NULL` for market orders. #' @return (data.table) the accepted order: #' - **order_id** (character): exchange order id. #' - **status** (character): order status. #' - **quantity** (numeric): accepted size. #' - **datetime** (POSIXct): acceptance time. #' @export submit_order <- function(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit = NULL) {   assert_args_submit_order(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit)   # generated   result <- ...   return(assert_return_submit_order(result))                      # generated } # Generated by roxyassert -- do not edit by hand.  assert_args_submit_order <- function(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit) {   assert::assert_scalar_character(symbol)   assert::assert_value_in_set(side, ORDER_SIDE)   assert::assert_scalar_numeric(quantity)   if (!is.null(price_limit)) assert::assert_scalar_numeric(price_limit)   invisible(NULL) }  assert_return_submit_order <- function(value) {   assert::assert_data_table(value)   assert::assert_column_types(value, "character", c("order_id", "status"))   assert::assert_column_types(value, "numeric", "quantity")   assert::assert_column_types(value, "POSIXct", "datetime")   return(value)   # returned untouched, so you can wrap it in return()/invisible() }

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Annotate tag (data.table) ((data.frame)), declare columns markdown bullet list exact form: bullet - **name** (type): description becomes column assertion. Column type uses base types . every column checked atomic type, list-columns rejected construction — nested/list column fails declared type. bullet syntax human-readable column documentation already write, docs generated schema come one place.
code: #' @return (data.table) open orders: #' - **order_id** (character): exchange order id. #' - **price** (numeric): limit price. #' - **datetime** (POSIXct): submission time.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Two helpers, optional. assert_args_<fn> emitted function least one typed @param; assert_return_<fn> @return carries parseable type. Functions typed tags get nothing. Explicit arguments. assert_args_<fn>(arg1, arg2, ...) takes documented parameters name, declaration order — call explicitly contract visible call site. (change function’s arguments, re-run document() update call.) Returns passed . assert_return_<fn>(value) validates returns value unchanged, can write return(assert_return_fn(x)) keep invisible(). invisible() irrelevant. Whether function returns visibly invisibly print concern, contract — generation depends solely typed @return. @return invisible(self) written prose skipped; annotate @return (R6<Engine>) assert . R6 methods scoped class. method submit class Engine generates assert_args_Engine__submit (double-underscore separator), two classes sharing method name — method free function name — never collide. Collision-safe. two generated names ever clash, generation aborts clear error rather silently overwriting. Internal, undocumented, committed. Generated helpers exported carry .Rd; live single R/contracts-generated.R (--edit banner) commit like NAMESPACE. package generates uses .

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Plain functions: yes R6 class methods: yes S4 / Reference classes: yet.

what: Why not put the type in the code instead?
title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Packages like typed declare types inside function body custom operator. works checks runtime, changes every function written. roxyassert keeps R code exactly treats documentation already write contract.
